title: chrysanthemum × morifolium
intro: Chrysanthemum × morifolium, also known as the florist's chrysanthemum or herb chrysanthemum, is a hybrid plant derived from the species Chrysanthemum grandiflorum and Chrysanthemum japonicum, primarily cultivated for its medicinal and ornamental value. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, prized for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other bioactive compounds. Regular consumption of Chrysanthemum × morifolium tea or supplements is believed to provide several health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often combined with other medicinal herbs, such as ginseng and licorice root, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that promote overall well-being and vitality.
